modal-basic-flow.title: Bare modal flow
modal-basic-flow.description: A basic scenario for `GrModal`: a minimal container, opening by button and explicit closing from user content.

modal-backdrop-guard.title: Backdrop guard for critical flows
modal-backdrop-guard.description: Shows `closeOnBackdrop=false` for cases where you must not accidentally lose draft or confirmation progress.

modal-size-switcher.title: Size variants for different payloads
modal-size-switcher.description: Isolates the effect of `size` on layout: the same entry point can open a compact review or a wide review panel.

modal-dialog-service.title: Imperative dialogs from an open modal
modal-dialog-service.description: Launch `useDialogService` (`confirm` / `alert` / `prompt`) straight from an open `GrModal`. The service mounts its own host in `document.body` on top of the window, so closing a dialog does not close the source modal â€” the decision is returned via a `Promise`.

basic.shellBody: `GrModal` handles the overlay, focus trap and panel sizing â€” you assemble the content yourself.

guarded.hint: Try clicking the backdrop: the modal stays open until the user picks an explicit action.
guarded.protectionBody: Use this mode for wizard/confirm flows where a draft must not be lost by accident.
guarded.unsavedChanges: Unsaved changes: pricing rules, SLA exceptions, recipients.

size.body: The same flow can scale for review, preview or a multi-column payload.

service.intro: An open `GrModal` invokes the imperative `useDialogService`. The service mounts its own host in `document.body` on top of the modal, so closing confirm/alert/prompt does not close the source window â€” it stays open, and the user's decision is returned through a `Promise`.
service.settingsBody: Launch service dialogs straight from the open window â€” it stays in place after any of them is closed.
service.empty: Empty for now â€” invoke any dialog above.
service.alertMessage: Changes were saved in the background. The settings window stayed open.
